input.headline: ‘My husband collects pictures of old men’s faces to give me’: Keira Knightley on art, ghosts and West Ham’s midfield

raw.headline: ‘My husband collects pictures of old men’s faces to give me’: Keira Knightley on art, ghosts and West Ham’s midfield

winner: Keira Knightley says she loves her husband's collection of old men's faces so much, she keeps one in her pocket just for when she feels like a ghost from West Ham's midfield.

candidates.text: My husband's collection is very specific—only men who look like they’ve been deep-fried in sadness since 1987.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison with a vivid image, fits the context well and is concise."}

candidates.text: I told my wife I collected portraits—she said I was just trying to get her into old men’s home decor.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks the specific punch of a surprising twist. It's mildly funny and concise, but doesn't fully align with the context or deliver maximum surprise."}

candidates.text: What's the difference between Keira Knightley's husband collecting old men's faces and a detective solving a cold case? One involves more aging out of the subject matter.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'aging out of the subject matter,' but it's a bit forced and lacks the punchiness needed for maximum humor. The comparison to a detective solving a cold case feels a bit generic and doesn't fully align with the specificity of the original headline."}

candidates.text: Why did Keira Knightley break up with her husband's face collection? She said, "I don't need a man of my dreams... I need a *midfield* partner!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke connects Keira Knightley's husband's face collection with West Ham's midfield, which is specific and somewhat surprising. However, the pun on 'midfield partner' feels a bit forced and lacks natural flow, reducing its overall humor impact."}
